root_text.an5.221:1.1: “Pañcime, bhikkhave, ādīnavā dīghacārikaṁ anavatthacārikaṁ anuyuttassa viharato.
root_text.an5.221:1.3: Assutaṁ na suṇāti, sutaṁ na pariyodāpeti, sutenekaccena avisārado hoti, gāḷhaṁ rogātaṅkaṁ phusati, na ca mittavā hoti.
root_text.an5.221:1.4: Ime kho, bhikkhave, pañca ādīnavā dīghacārikaṁ anavatthacārikaṁ anuyuttassa viharato.
root_text.an5.221:2.3: Assutaṁ suṇāti, sutaṁ pariyodāpeti, sutenekaccena visārado hoti, na gāḷhaṁ rogātaṅkaṁ phusati, mittavā ca hoti.
root_text.an5.221:2.4: Ime kho, bhikkhave, pañca ānisaṁsā samavatthacāre”ti.

translation_text.an5.221:1.1: “Mendicants, there are these five drawbacks for someone who likes long and aimless wandering.
translation_text.an5.221:1.3: You don’t learn new things. You don’t clarify what you’ve learned. You lack confidence in some things you have learned. You contract a severe illness. You don’t have any friends.
translation_text.an5.221:1.4: These are the five drawbacks for someone who likes long and aimless wandering.
translation_text.an5.221:2.1: There are these five benefits of a reasonable amount of wandering.
translation_text.an5.221:2.3: You learn new things. You clarify what you’ve learned. You have confidence in some things you have learned. You don’t contract severe illness. You have friends.
translation_text.an5.221:2.4: These are the five benefits of a reasonable amount of wandering.”
